danger_signals.visual_form: fast-moving enemy ship sprites on screen
danger_signals.meaning: active threat requiring immediate player targeting and destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: asteroid sprites scattered across play area
danger_signals.meaning: environmental hazard that damages shield on collision or failed shot

danger_signals.visual_form: bomb sprite objects moving toward player position
danger_signals.meaning: incoming attack that must be destroyed before impact
guided or ballistic threat requiring rapid targeting

danger_signals.visual_form: shield meter visual degradation (inferred from mechanic description)
danger_signals.meaning: player vulnerability increasing with each missed shot; visual indicator of mounting danger

danger_signals.visual_form: final boss alien entity sprite at Earth level
danger_signals.meaning: ultimate existential threat; alien antagonist attempting planetary invasion

safety_signals.visual_form: allied ship sprite distinct from enemy vessels
safety_signals.meaning: non-target entity; must avoid destruction; delivers power-up items

safety_signals.visual_form: power-up item sprite from friendly craft
safety_signals.meaning: restorative resource that restores player shield integrity

safety_signals.visual_form: special power-up sprite with comet visual motif

safety_signals.meaning: emergency defensive capability eliminating all current threats on screen

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Triton base on Neptune serves as launch point; represents human military alliance

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: spacecraft design distinct from friendly vessels; final boss Nemesis is Ragossian alien entity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: enemy ships establish visual 'cordon' between player and Earth; invasive presence throughout solar system levels
Earth as objective/destination; represents human civilization and political authority requiring defense

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: enemy Ragossian spacecraft saturating solar system levels (Uranus, Saturn, Jupiter progressions)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: increasing enemy density and difficulty scaling through level progression toward Earth; final boss confrontation represents invasion apex

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: player spacecraft positioned between Triton base and Earth; medicine vials as objective collectable (inferred)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player progression through sequential planets represents courier mission execution; delivery objective drives spatial-temporal narrative

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Earth level as final stage; Nemesis positioned at planetary boundary
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: boss battle at atmosphere represents last defensive line; player must prevent alien penetration of final sanctuary

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: shield degradation through gameplay (missed shots); constant resource management visual feedback
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: time-limited mission context creates escalating tension; shield depletion mechanic forces aggressive engagement and rapid decision-making

environmental_storytelling.example: level sequence (Uranus → Saturn → Jupiter → Earth) traces courier mission path from Triton base toward destination, visualizing spatial journey through solar system
end-of-level boss fights at each planet represent Ragossian military hierarchy; final Nemesis encounter at Earth demonstrates invasion command structure
medicine vial delivery goal embedded in level design; retrieval from Triton and delivery to Earth frames entire game spatially
increasing enemy spacecraft, asteroid, and projectile volume through progression levels visualizes Ragossian invasion intensity scaling toward planetary core

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: fast movement patterns requiring quick player reflexes; continuous firing/projectile generation toward player
delivers power-up drops (shield repair, protection comet, screen-clear bomb) to player; avoidance of destruction necessary
static or drifting threat; damages player shield on failed targeting; no active aggression but passive obstruction
attempts penetration of Earth atmosphere; represents culmination of Ragossian invasion force; requires defeat to save human civilization
continuously depletes with each missed shot; visual indicator of mounting vulnerability; restoration via friendly power-up drops

notes: To the Earth uses light gun paradigm to communicate world-state primarily through enemy encounter density, spatial progression, and resource management feedback. Faction identity relies on spacecraft visual distinction rather than color-coding; enemy Ragossians identified through opposition role and alien boss entity. World-state (bacteriological invasion crisis, time pressure, final Earth defense) manifests through level progression structure, enemy escalation, and shield depletion mechanic. Environmental storytelling achieved through planetary journey sequence and boss encounters representing invasion hierarchy. The game communicates danger through enemy speed/density and safety through friendly power-up deliveries and defensive items. Political context (Terrestrial Federation vs. Ragossian invaders) implicit in narrative framing rather than explicit visual faction markers.
